video_title: Top #DDD Videos in Maryland with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network

restaurants.name: Grano Pasta Bar
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in Baltimore, Maryland, about two miles from downtown in the Hamden neighborhood. Now, we're right in the center of all the universities. Now, I hear that they've got a place where you sit at the counter as the chefs cook. The dude that owns it is from Italy, and the place is legit. This is Grano Pasta Bar. At only 400 square feet, this joint's been making room for just one thing since Naples-born chef Gino Troia opened in 08.

dishes.description: Clam dish with a lovely light sauce, a touch of wine, spinach, and tomatoes, served with pasta.
dishes.guy_response: You better deliver, and you sure delivered, man. That is awesome. Fantastic. Just has a great amount of herbs in the sauce.

dishes.description: Handmade ravioli with a cream sauce, wild mushrooms, and sprinkles of truffles on top.
dishes.guy_response: It's rich. It's light. It's earthy. That's outrageous. I mean, you hand make everything. The pasta, the filling. Delicious dish. Thank you. Outrageous. It just almost melts in your mouth. When you taste it, the mushrooms and the natural flavors come through.

restaurants.name: Miss Shirley's Cafe
restaurants.guy_intro: So, here I am in the Roland Park neighborhood of Baltimore, Maryland on Cold Spring Lane. Well, more fondly known as Eddie Dobkin Way. That's because back in the 90s, Eddie had like five rockin' restaurants in this area. And the one he's most widely known for is a funky big breakfast joint known as Miss Shirley's Cafe.

dishes.description: Two large pieces of challah bread stuffed with an amazing coconut cream.
dishes.guy_response: Each bite you get the texture of the coconut, the sweetness with the crunch. I have no problem giving it a wow-wee. And, uh... Did she just drop a wow-wee on me? Wow-wee. It's amazing. Apparently that's something you might have heard when Eddie was around. One of his favorite expressions was wow-wee. So this guy was a fan of hers. And... This guy was alive at the party. This is a wow-wee.

dishes.description: Jumbo lump crab meat crab cake, made with Old Bay, mayo, eggs, mustard, salt, roasted red peppers, parsley, and cracker meal, served on a sandwich with Tabasco bacon onion jam, lettuce, tomatoes, and Old Bay remoulade.

restaurants.name: Di Pasquale's
restaurants.guy_intro: E Pasquale in Baltimore is a takeout deli. Turned sit down joint. For home cooked favorites. I'll come here, get some nice pasta, salads, and things like that. Definitely some of the best food I've ever had. I've never had a lasagna like this. Out of this world. I feel like I'm at. from Italy, right?

dishes.description: Arborio rice balls stuffed with a beef and pork mixture, marinara, and mozzarella, then floured, breaded, and fried until golden brown. Served with marinara sauce.
dishes.guy_response: The rice has got a nice little firm center to it with a nice creaminess on the outside. That is just fantastic. I love the peas in there too. That's about it right there. Winner, winner, rice ball dinner. First thing that hits me is the crispiness of the outer crust. The meat and the rice and the mozzarella all blend together. It's really good.

restaurants.name: Broadway Diner
restaurants.guy_intro: So this leg of the trip, you rode in and sent me to Baltimore, to the kind of places we love doing on Triple D. A big, shiny, family owned diner serving homemade everything from everywhere. Hey, welcome to the Broadway Diner.

dishes.description: A seafood dish featuring scallops, lobster tails, shrimp, and mussels, cooked with white wine, garlic, fresh tomato, basil, and marinara sauce, served over al dente pasta with cheese.
dishes.guy_response: The pasta's al dente. I go to Italian restaurants across the country, I can't find pasta that's al dente. I have nothing to say, man, other than just wow. Delicious. That's pretty much it in a word.

restaurants.name: Stony Creek Inn
restaurants.guy_intro: When you're in Maryland and you're thinking traditional food, well, you're probably thinking crab, especially the way they steam them and dump a pile of them on butcher paper and everybody dives in. So I'm 20 minutes south of Baltimore rolling in for what they call a crab feast at a place called the Stony Creek Inn.

dishes.description: Blue crabs steamed by the basket with a custom seasoning blend (salt, paprika, dry mustard, chili flake, cayenne, thyme) and served with sweet corn.
dishes.guy_response: They're very rich. It's buttery. It's sweet. The salt compliments it really well. The seasoning on the crab is pretty outstanding. Wow.

restaurants.name: G&A Restaurant
restaurants.guy_intro: In 1927, a Greek immigrant by the name of Gregory Theokoumakos opened a hot dog stand in Baltimore serving Coney Island-style dogs with his own chili sauce recipe. Now, it was a hit. I mean, a big hit. And 81 years later, it still is. And it all began with owner Andy Ferrantos' great uncle Gregory, who partnered up with his cousin Alex and started a tradition.

dishes.description: Hot dog loaded with homemade Coney sauce (chili), onions, and ketchup, served on a steamed bun.
dishes.guy_response: Mm. That's great. Nice and juicy dog. Crazy meat concoction on top. That's good. That's good. That's good. That's good. That's good. That's good. White onion's perfect. Bun's nice and steamed. It'll cost $1.90. That's it. If I was a college student, I could live here. You taste the hot dog. You taste the onions. You taste the chili. Delicious. It's the best hot dog I've ever had.

dishes.description: French fries served Baltimore style with homemade gravy.
dishes.guy_response: Can I try one? There's nothing wrong with that at all. That's right. I'd dip the dog in that.

restaurants.name: Chaps Pit Beef
restaurants.guy_intro: This is the famous Chaps? This is Chaps. Look at that. If you come to Baltimore, this is what you better see. If you don't, it's not pit beef. It's charcoal pit beef. You're not going to get it nowhere else.

dishes.description: Bottom round beef roasted over charcoal, sliced thin, and piled high on a roll, served with white onion, salt, pepper, and bright horseradish.
dishes.guy_response: You know, I like the little char to it. But I got to say, the medley of the white onion, the really nice, bright horseradish, a little bit of salt and pepper, a great roll, not too much bread. I hate when you get too much bread and it starts to become... It's all about the meat. Right. I'll just clean you right out, won't it? What? Wow. That was all horseradish. It's good for you. It's good for you. Whoa. There it is. My toes are tingling. I mean, seriously. I go like, wow. Welcome to Baltimore. It's spicy. It's moist. The meat is so tender. I give it way past a 10.

dishes.description: A massive custom sandwich featuring pit beef, jalapenos, horseradish, ham, burger, hash, nacho cheese sauce, sausage, pickles, chopped beef, and turkey.
dishes.guy_response: Oh, he goes for a double bite. What cheese? Is there anything right here? You got... No, you look fine.
